stories.headline: Iran War: Vance to Lead Iran Talks as Tehran Says Ceasefire Violated | Daybreak Europe 4/9/2026
stories.summary:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. And we'll tell you what matters for investors in Europe, giving you insight before trading begins.

On today's show, JD Vance will lead US-Iran talks in Islamabad this weekend even as fighting continues in the Middle East. Tehran claims the ceasefire has been

stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump threatens action if Tehran fails to comply with deal
stories.summary: UN human rights chief Volker Turk describes Israel's attacks on Lebanon as 'horrific', coming so soon after ceasefire.

stories.headline: Ceasefire threatened as Iran closes strait again and Trump warns US troops to remain
stories.summary: In a social media post, Trump said the surge of US warships and troops will remain around Iran “until such time as the real agreement reached is fully complied with.”

stories.headline: The Fragile Cease-Fire in Iran
stories.summary: President Trump announced a conditional cease-fire with Israel on Iran, but the fundamental issues that led to the war remain unresolved.

stories.headline: Trump slams NATO over Iran after meeting Rutte, renews Greenland threat
stories.summary: US president meets NATO chief, expresses disappointment over member states failing to back war on Iran.

stories.headline: Newsletter: NATO holds after Rutte-Trump talks amid fragile ceasefire with Iran
stories.summary: Also in this newsletter: After US-Iran ceasefire deal, Europe faces tough questions.

stories.headline: Why oil and gas prices could stay high in Europe even if the Iran war ends
stories.summary: Energy prices in Europe could stay high for a while, even though the region doesn’t rely heavily on the Strait of Hormuz. Here’s what’s behind it.

stories.headline: Europe Today: Ceasefire in Iran at risk as Israeli strikes kill over 200 in Lebanon
stories.summary: Tune in to Europe Today, Euronews' flagship morning programme at 8 am Brussels time. In just 20 minutes, we bring you up to speed on the biggest news of the day.

stories.headline: 'NATO in grave danger after Iran war,' former US NATO ambassador tells Euronews
stories.summary: US President Trump's frequent threats to leave NATO, as well as the US army's heavy use of expensive, military assets in Iran has weakened the alliance, former US ambassador to NATO tells Euronews.

stories.headline: German Industry Production Disappointed Even Before Iran War
stories.summary: German industrial production unexpectedly fell in February, casting doubt on a swift recovery in Europe’s largest economy even before the Iran war started.

stories.headline: He's Australia's most decorated soldier. Now he's at the centre of a historic war crimes case
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith's case is not only unprecedented for Australia but "extraordinary" for the globe too, scholars say.

stories.headline: Hundreds search for wolf that escaped from zoo in South Korea

stories.headline: Thomas Peterffy on IBKR's Plan to Professionalize Prediction Markets
stories.summary: A legendary trading entrepreneur says prediction markets are the next big thing.

stories.headline: Odd Lots: Thomas Peterffy on IBKR’s Prediction Markets (Podcast)
stories.summary: Right now, when you think about prediction markets, you basically think about two main companies: Polymarket and Kalshi. And then when you think of what’s being traded on those platforms, there’s a whole range of stuff from elections to sports to real economic outcomes, to totally gonzo random stuff, like who will win the next season of Big Brother. So far, sports is where a lot of the money is. But at least in theory, prediction markets could be

stories.headline: Thomas Peterffy Says There Should Be No Bans on Insider Trading
stories.summary: Thomas Peterffy, the billionaire founder and chairman of Interactive Brokers Group Inc., has an answer to the insider-trading concerns dogging prediction markets: stop trying to prevent it — for the good of society.

stories.headline: Iran Envoy Deletes Post on Negotiators’ Arrival to Pakistan
stories.summary: Iran’s Ambassador to Pakistan Reza Amiri Moghadam appeared to have deleted a post on X that said his country’s delegation would arrive in Islamabad on Thursday night ahead of talks with the US this weekend.

stories.headline: Architect Norman Foster on Hong Kong's Ever-Changing Skyline
stories.summary: The legendary architect sits down with Bloomberg to discuss Hong Kong's cityscape and his vision for the future. He's visiting Hong Kong to celebrate the 40th anniversary of the HSBC building in the city. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Horizons Middle East & Africa 4/9/2026
stories.summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: India’s Generic Copies Dent Eli Lilly’s Sales
stories.summary: The flood of cheap copies of Novo Nordisk A/S’ blockbuster drug semaglutide in India is already reshaping the country’s fast-growing obesity market, cutting into Eli Lilly & Co.’s early lead and offering a first real test of how Novo may fare as its patents expire worldwide.

stories.headline: Dangote Targets $40 Billion Investment in Africa Expansion Drive
stories.summary: The Dangote Group will need at least $40 billion of investment to fund a growth drive over the next five years that includes quadrupling fertilizer output and more than doubling its oil refinery capacity, the African Export-Import Bank said.

stories.headline: Trafigura Signs Offtake, Financing Deal With Ghanaian Gold Miner
stories.summary: Trafigura Group signed an offtake agreement with Ghana’s Heath Goldfields Ltd. to purchase 700,000 ounces of gold doré, while also providing debt financing, as it pushes further into precious metals markets.

stories.headline: Galp Energia Buys Wind Portfolio in Spain in €320 Million Deal
stories.summary: Galp Energia SGPS SA has agreed to acquire an onshore wind portfolio in Spain from Helia Funds in a transaction valued at €320 million ($373 million), as Portugal’s largest oil company accelerates efforts to diversify its clean energy portfolio beyond solar.

stories.headline: TAP Posts Fourth Straight Profit as Sale Process Gains Pace
stories.summary: TAP SA reported a fourth consecutive annual profit in 2025, as the carrier pushes to expand its network and draw in a strategic investor.

stories.headline: Clean Energy Team Wins Salt River Project Election in Arizona
stories.summary: Proponents of renewable power will control the Phoenix area utility’s policymaking for the first time after they won an unusually contentious race that drew attention from national groups.

stories.headline: No Charges for Wisconsin Mayor Who Removed Ballot Drop Box in 2024
stories.summary: A special prosecutor said a review of the incident found there was not sufficient evidence to charge Doug Diny, the mayor of Wausau.

stories.headline: Three life terms for Kashmir’s Aasiya Andrabi fit India’s ‘broader pattern’
stories.summary: Legal experts say the 64-year-old grandmother's harsh conviction is mainly based on offensive speech-making.

stories.headline: Fiscal discipline as Ukraine's real test on the road to EU membership
stories.summary: Fiscal discipline will be Ukraine's real test on the road to EU membership, writes Danylo Hetmantsev, chair of parliament’s finance, tax and customs committee, in an op-ed for Euronews. He argues that Ukraine has already shown it can turn shadow economy reforms into real revenue.

stories.headline: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.summary: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.headline: Gold Steadies as Traders Weigh Fragile Ceasefire in Iran War
stories.summary: Gold steadied after two days of gains as traders weighed the prospect of a diplomatic resolution to the Iran war, even as sporadic fighting threatened to derail a fragile ceasefire.

stories.headline: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
stories.summary: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

stories.headline: JD Vance says Iran would be ‘dumb’ to let talks collapse over Lebanon
stories.summary: The US vice president says Iran had a 'legitimate misunderstanding' about Lebanon's inclusion in this week's ceasefire.

stories.summary: Ukraine’s president tells podcast he has tried to draw White House’s attention to collaboration between Moscow and Tehran over strikes on US basesHow the Ukraine and Iran wars are starting to overlapMiddle East crisis – live updatesThe US has ignored compelling evidence that Russia has been helping Iran to target US bases in the Middle East because it “trusts” Vladimir Putin, according to the Ukrainian president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy.Speaking in a

stories.headline: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.summary: Pakistan has a historic relationship with Iran, but an agreement was far from certain, writes the BBC’s Caroline Davies

stories.headline: Has Trump Met His War Goals in Iran?
stories.summary: On the first day of the pause, Iran fired missiles and launched drones in the region. It said an oil refinery on Lavan Island had been attacked. Israel continued its strikes in Lebanon.

stories.headline: On Truth Social, Trump Supporters Fume About Iran War
stories.summary: A growing chorus of disaffected Trump supporters is sounding off in the replies to his posts on the social media platform he founded.

stories.headline: As Trump Swings on Iran War, Republican Leaders in Congress Are Largely Silent
stories.summary: In a week in which President Trump has veered from threatening to wipe out Iranian civilization to declaring a cease-fire, Congress is out of session and lawmakers with the power to declare war are mostly in the dark.

stories.headline: What Trump Has Accomplished in Iran After 5 Weeks of War
stories.summary: President Trump said that “we have already met and exceeded” his military objectives. But his goals are largely unresolved.

stories.headline: Trump administration signals it is mulling NATO withdrawal after Iran war
stories.summary: The US president has lashed out at European partners for declining to contribute military forces to the war on Iran.

stories.headline: JD Vance claims US is not interfering in Hungary election
stories.summary: US vice-president says on visit to Budapest ‘we had to show’ support for Viktor Orbán, as opposition leads polls Hungary elections: what is at stake and who is likely to win?JD Vance has pushed back against claims that the US is interfering in Hungarian politics, describing the accusations as “darkly ironic”, as a set of polls suggested the opposition Tisza party could win a supermajority in the forthcoming elections.After spending his first day

stories.headline: Trump-backed Republican Clay Fuller wins election to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene
stories.summary: He will serve out the rest of Marjorie Taylor Greene's term, which ends in January 2027.

stories.headline: Top Australian soldier charged with war crimes to remain in jail on remand
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith faces five charges of the war crime of murder, which has a penalty of life in prison.

stories.headline: Bondi Won’t Appear on Capitol Hill for Scheduled Epstein Deposition
stories.summary: Pam Bondi had already been working to avoid testifying before she was fired as attorney general. The House Oversight Committee said she would not honor her subpoena because she was no longer in the post.

stories.headline: Bondi won’t testify before House about Epstein files, DOJ says
stories.summary: Some House members say they still intend to pursue their subpoena of former attorney general Pam Bondi, despite her firing last week.

stories.headline: Republican wins Marjorie Taylor Greene seat but by tighter margin
stories.summary: Clay Fuller, backed by President Donald Trump, defeated Democrat Shawn Harris by a much smaller margin than what Republicans enjoyed in previous years in the deep-red district vacated by Marjorie Taylor Greene.

stories.headline: Gambia appoints British barrister to prosecute gruesome Jammeh-era crimes
stories.summary: Ex-President Yahya Jammeh's 22-year rule was known for enforced disappearances and extrajudicial killings.

stories.headline: White House Secures Foreign Steel for Trump’s Ballroom Project
stories.summary: ArcelorMittal, a European steel maker, is donating tens of millions of dollars of foreign steel for President Trump’s new ballroom.

stories.summary: New administration reverses expropriation of property founded by ex-Nazi Paul Schäfer, leaving victims in limboWith its Germanic crosses and colourful toy-town facades, the village square of the tiny Chilean settlement of Villa Baviera gives little indication of the horrors of its past.Until 1991, this cattle town of a few hundred people was a compound known as Colonia Dignidad. Its leader, Paul Schäfer, a former Nazi and weapons smuggler, bought

stories.headline: Greece announces social media ban for under-15s, citing anxiety and sleep problems
stories.summary: PM says ban will come into force in January if it is backed by parliament and calls for united action across EUGreece has announced a social media ban for under-15s from 1 January, with the country’s prime minister, Kyriakos Mitsotakis, citing rising anxiety, sleep problems and the addictive design of online platforms – although he acknowledged it may incur the wrath of some children.“We have decided to go ahead with a difficult but necessary mea

stories.headline: Turkey puts 11 leaders of LGBTQ+ rights association on trial for 'obscenity'
stories.summary: Homosexuality is not illegal in Turkey, but the LGBTQ+ community is frequently targeted by authorities who blame it for the country's declining birth rate.

stories.headline: Vance says he prays U.S. is ‘on God’s side’ in Iran war
stories.summary: When asked whether he shared Trump’s view that the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran carried divine backing, the vice president declined to go as far.

stories.headline: WHO suspends Gaza medical evacuations after contractor killed by Israeli troops
stories.summary: Israel's military says troops fired at a vehicle driven by the Palestinian because they believed it posed "an immediate threat".

stories.headline: After clashes with Hegseth, Army secretary says he isn’t going anywhere
stories.summary: Dan Driscoll, praised by the White House, said in a statement to The Washington Post that “I have no plans to depart or resign as the Secretary of the Army.”

stories.headline: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads
stories.summary: The ruling party has unveiled a draft law that strips voters of the chance to elect the president.

stories.headline: JD Vance backs Orbán's re-election bid in Budapest visit and hits out at EU
stories.summary: US Vice-President JD Vance intervenes in the campaign to give Orbán a ringing endorsement in Budapest.

stories.headline: Ringleader of suspected human trafficking network arrested in Ethiopia
stories.summary: Yetbarek Dawit is alleged to have operated a multi-million-dollar operation, using torture to raise money.

stories.headline: Vietnam's leader To Lam strengthens power in unanimous assembly vote
stories.summary: Vietnam's communist party chief is now also the country's president - an unusual concentration of power.

stories.headline: The US refinery now processing Venezuelan oil
stories.summary: Chevron is now importing 250,000 barrels of crude per day from Venezuela.

stories.headline: Carnival fever hits Lagos as locals celebrate Afro-Brazilian heritage
stories.summary: The centuries-old Fanti Carnival returned to Nigeria on Monday with a burst of colour and spectacle.

stories.headline: Fake Australian, Chinese and Brazilian police stations: BBC goes inside a seized scam compound

stories.headline: Spain's huge pork industry seeks salvation from swine fever threat

stories.headline: AI videos fuel rhetoric as Orbán bids for four more years in Hungary
stories.summary: Videos have targeted Viktor Orbán's election rival, who could unseat him after 16 years in office.

stories.headline: Faced with new energy shock, Europe asks if reviving nuclear is the answer
stories.summary: As war drives up gas and fuel prices, Europeans turn again to the issue of energy independence.

stories.headline: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
stories.summary: There were some glaring omissions in the US president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

stories.headline: Fears cost of water and beer to soar as India's scorching summer hits
stories.summary: Makers of plastic and glass bottles are struggling to access raw materials as the war squeezes supplies.

stories.headline: Macron calls Trump's remarks on his marriage 'inelegant'
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron has said Donald Trump's comments about his marriage were "neither elegant nor up to standard".
